# LazyReel

> LazyReel is a free MCP server that decoded 21B+ views of real short-form video (TikTok and Instagram, from the past 6 months) so a coding agent already knows what is breaking out in a niche before it writes anything. It is video research for AI, not AI for video creation. It pairs with a video-generation MCP like Higgsfield: LazyReel decides what is worth making and hands over the brief, the gen MCP renders it.

Key facts an agent should know:

- What it is: a stdio MCP server plus a companion skill set. It gives a coding agent (Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or any MCP client) 17 tools: 16 skills plus a status tool.
- Positioning: research and briefs, not raw generation. It decides what to make and why, grounded in real decoded video. Pair it with a video-gen MCP (for example Higgsfield) to actually render the video.
- The data: real TikTok and Instagram videos from the past 6 months, decoded by an in-house pipeline. The more recent the upload, the stronger the signal; the last 30 days are weighted highest. The corpus grows every day.
- Price: free for agents. No account, no card. The token only authorizes read-only research and cannot spend money, post, or touch private data.
- Anti-slop thesis: if the output looks AI-generated (the word "revolutionary", the rocket emoji, feature-list hooks, studio-ad polish), it has failed. The skills are tuned to kill those tells.

## The 17 tools

Read the niche:
- niche_decode: structured read on what is working in a niche (hook patterns, dominant visual approach, real breakout teardowns, trending tags).
- find_trends: what is breaking out right now, the formats and hooks over-indexing this week.
- study_examples: pull real decoded videos by niche, format, or hook pattern.
- search_corpus: search the decoded-video library by niche, format, or hook pattern.
- content_gaps: supply-versus-demand map; the under-used patterns that over-perform are the opening.
- app_insights: the app-ad vertical; hook by format patterns and view-breakout lift for mobile-app ads.

Reverse-engineer a winner:
- format_teardown: a winning video's narrative DNA, edit DNA, signature device, and how to rebuild it.
- viral_teardowns: real breakouts diagnosed for why they out-reached the creator's following.
- winning_combos: the hook by format pairings that over-index in breakouts, with the lift.
- breakout_vs_dud: why the same concept gets 1K vs 1M views, validated on concept-matched and same-creator pairs.
- format_playbook: a named format turned into a repeatable, shootable step-by-step.

Make the thing:
- video_ideas: scroll-worthy concepts, each on a named framework, mapped to the awareness ladder.
- cracked_hooks: ranked hooks, each a different pattern, caption-safe, brand named late.
- shoot_brief: a full brief with framework, angle, hook bank, a beat-by-beat table (VO, on-screen text, B-roll), CTA, and shoot notes.
- replicate_format: take a winning format and rebuild it end to end for your product.

Keep it honest:
- kill_the_slop: name what is wrong with weak copy against the anti-slop bar, then rewrite it sharper.
- get_status: says plainly what is live and what is not.

## How references work

The repository commits a curated list of real example videos (`mcp/data/examples.json`, 418 videos with their public TikTok URLs and decoded labels) plus derived teardowns and aggregates. The tools study_examples, search_corpus, and viral_teardowns read that list to point the agent at real videos. The raw scraped dataset and the downloaded video files are not committed, only the public links and the derived analysis.

## Links

- Install (free token, one prompt, any MCP client): https://lazyreel-site.vercel.app/mcp-install.html
- Source and the full MCP, skills, and decoded-insight wiki: https://github.com/dylanpakd-cyber/lazyreel
- Works with: Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, or any MCP client.
- Pairs with: a video-generation MCP such as Higgsfield.
